items.title: Family Jewels
items.summary: Bas Rutten is a hero of mine. I don't have too…
items.content_html:
Bas Rutten is a hero of mine.

I don't have too many of those.

But Bas is one.

And, he made a series of MMA videos back in the day.

Even a how to protect yourself in the streets video.

It's both hilarious and educational.

Highly recommend.

Anyways, in this particular self-defense video he goes on and on about the virtues of a kick to the groin.

How simple it is.

How effective.

How we should use it.

Everybody underestimates a kick to the groin.

Meaning, all things being unequal, a kick to the nuggets tends to even things out.

May the nads be ever in your favor.

Now, as a old(ish) man of peace(ish), I would never tell you to kick someone in the chicken tenders, but do so if you need to.

Do so if you must.

And that's a good rule right?

Fight honorably.

Until you need to not.

If you're in a struggle that seems like you winning means you get to live, remember to fight dirty from the start.

What's fighting dirty?

Well, basically take a look at what you cannot do in an organized, professional, fight.

And do a whole lotta that.

Piledriving. Eye-gouging. Headbutts. Throat. Using objects. Leverage. Back of the head.

Read the list of no-no's and make 'em yes-yes's.

Only in defense of course.

When lives are at stake.

Of yourself or others.

And only if you are at a disadvantage.

Fight fair until it's not.

Oh, and it's never fair.

🦶🍒

Ring the bells.

items.title: Lost and Found
items.summary: Why do they call it fall in love? Shouldn't it be…
items.content_html:
Why do they call it fall in love?

Shouldn't it be rise?

Rise in love?

Soar in love?

No, fall's right.

Love is a strength and a weakness.

I've said this before.

And I'll say it again.

Love is a strength and a weakness.

It makes you stronger and weaker at the same time.

When you love something or someone, you have a duality at play.

An infinite helix of affection intertwined with concern.

Joy and sorrow.

Jubilance at birth.

Mourned at death.

Everything in between in between.

Love is the most powerful thing in the universe.

More powerful than hate.

You've been powered by both.

What say you?

So you have a choice:

To love or not to love.

And for many years, I chose the latter.

So I would remain strong.

But that was weakness talking.

Love's weakness is one I cannot put into words.

It can cripple you.

But the alternative is loneliness.

Emptiness.

Void.

Sure, it can all end the same way.

But better to have loved, I say.

Than to never have loved at all.

I'm missing something in the middle part.

But you understand.

May you know the love of loving someone more than yourself.

May you know the love of having been loved by someone more than they love themselves.

And may those loves fall in love.

And may you all always believe it was better to have done so.

Better to have loved...

items.title: 'Neath The Sunshine
items.summary: What do grandmasters do with their idle time? Their down time.
items.content_html:
What do grandmasters do with their idle time?

Their down time.

Their let their hair down time.

They probably meditate a lot.

Contemplate.

Ever tinkering on their grandmastery.

Do you remember the old show, The People's.Court?

It was Judge Judy and all them before all them.

Judge Wapner.

Judge Wapner was an old curmudgeon.

I am partial to them.

I remember one episode years ago where someone was on the show and kept calling Judge Wapner, "Your Majesty," instead of Your Honor.

"Yes, your majesty."

"No, I'm not royalty, call me your honor."

"I'm sorry your majesty, I mean, your honor!"

He kept doing it. Slipping up.

Which infuriated Judge Wapner at first, then he just found it funny.

Anger, then delight.

Which is, as we all know, the sweet spot.

I suppose I could find the clip online somewhere but I don't want to.

Because I know I'll have remembered a detail wrong or got the whole show wrong.

So I'll leave it in my brain.

As a childhood memory.

Hope all you mothers had a good day yesterday. 

And all days, while we're counting.

And we're always counting.

🎵 "One for sorrow, two for joy..." 🎵

items.title: Word To Your Mother
items.summary: Happy Mother's Day. To you if you're a mother. And to…
items.content_html:
Happy Mother's Day.

To you if you're a mother.

And to your mother.

Word.

And the mothers around you.

🤶

items.title: Strong Beginnings
items.summary: Weekends is such a bad name for the best days of…
items.content_html:
Weekends is such a bad name for the best days of the week.

Right?

Week.

Weak.

Ends.

It's all over.

So what should we call it instead?

Hmm.

Oh, it could be anything.

If only he put it somewhere kind of obvious.

Tee hee.

I amuse myself.

items.title: A Whisper
items.summary: I remember a comedian doing stand-up. Talking about applying for jobs.
items.content_html:
I remember a comedian doing stand-up.

Talking about applying for jobs.

Living in a rough neighborhood.

And how tough it is to find employment where he lives.

Finally, he lands an interview. For a restaurant position.

Sharing that he knew it was a bad idea when, in the middle of it, the interviewer looked him up and down and asked him, deadpan:

"Can you fight?"

Well, can you?

I haven't thought about that bit in a long arse time.

Still makes me chuckle.

It just popped into my head when I sat down here.

So here's another question.

More germane to the world.

Will you fight?

And when?

Are you doing it now?

Will you do it when a line is crossed?

And where is that line?

There's nothing sadder to me than watching a bully hitting an innocent, and that innocent laughs it off or pretends nothing's happening as the bully continues to strike.

Ever see that?

It's a sad thing to see.

99% of your anger is focused rightfully on the bully.

You dirty...

But there's 1%, 1% max, directed at the innocent.

What are you doing? Have you no backbone?! C'mon! FIGHT BACK!!!

Now, this is all theoretical, because, as you know, I am a old(ish) man of peace.

Peace.

Love.

✌

💛

All that jazz.

That's me.

Yeah right.

It is!

I think.

🤷‍♂️

Me no know anymore.

What are we talking about here?

And what's it sound like?

items.title: Take A Look At This
items.summary: Guys like to poke fun at one another. Bust each others'…
items.content_html:
Guys like to poke fun at one another.

Bust each others' burals.

You know, the nuggets?

⚾⚾

It's just something we do.

And when I was younger, we had a running joke.

We would say things like:

If this were an action movie, I'd be the guy putting my life in danger and fighting and bringing back data from the field for you to analyze.

We said it in crasser language.

But you get the idea.

It wasn't a joke where we picked on one person, we just used it when we wanted action.

Itching.

And to instill that sense in the other.

And let me tell you, it works.

No one wanted to be the data guy.

The back at base guy.

The numbers cruncher.

But they do pretty well, now.

Always have.

"Fifty dollar bill. Fifty dollar bill! 50 DOLLAR BILL!!!"

items.title: Loud And Opaque
items.summary: You know what I love? Writing in generalities when people need…
items.content_html:
You know what I love?

Writing in generalities when people need specifics.

👁👁

You might hear a lot of about adding value.

Just add value.

I sure do.

And you might be wondering, like I do, what that all means.

Well, I'll tell you.

It's just a way to sound smart.

To sound influency.

To sound like you're giving advice when all you're really doing is just being vague.

Add value is like a newer Always Be Closing.

ABC!

But forget all that.

You want to add value?

Help.

Help people.

Help animals.

Help your surroundings.

Help the universe.

Help causes you still believe in.

Here's hoping that includes yourself.

Just my value add for the day.

Invoice is in your inbox.

As you will see, rates have gone up.

Way up.

K thx bye.

items.title: Make That Change
items.summary: 🎵 Who's bad? 🎶 We live in this weird world, man.
items.content_html:
🎵 Who's bad? 🎶

We live in this weird world, man.

A strange universe.

To quote Billy Shat:

"And I love it."

But here's the thing.

A lot of the "heroes" my history classes taught me about are now taught to today's kids as villains.

A lot of icons becomes crooks.

Nothing changes but our perspective.

Just with the passage of time.

But, like all things, there are exceptions to the rule.

That prove the rule.

Sometimes, a villain can become a hero again.

Take Michael Jackson.

If there ever is a person I'm glad is having a comeback (of sorts) it's Michael.

The first MJ.

The best MJ.

I'm a bit ashamed to say when I was younger, MJ was a butt of a lot of jokes.

But he's had this odd redemption story.

Although that's not entirely fair, because it implies he did something wrong in the first place.

Which now, it seems, he never did.

That he was always good.

Now, the suspicious side of me is thinking, hmm, all this good stuff about MJ is coming out just coincidentally alongside the release of his movie.

But let's ignore that.

Put that thought in a corner and point my finger at it.

Stay.

...

But weird timing, right?

...

And focus on the positive.

It's ok to like Mike again.

You ever hear his real voice?

Nevermind.

I am just happy that we don't just stomp on graves anymore.

Some graves we can repair.

We can apologize to.

We can visit. 

We can remember.

Aww, MJ, the world did you dirty, man. 

I'm thinking this is the world's collective "our bad" moment.

And it's good to know, even in these absurd times, that we can admit we were wrong. 

A good, bad, man.

Hero.

Never the "e" word many wanted him to be.

🎵 You know I'm bad. 🎶

📻

Sorry for ever doubting.

To BadMFs.

Keeping the good good.

items.title: We're Both Learning Together
items.summary: Good people. Yes, I'm talking to you. You good? It occurs…
items.content_html:
Good people.

Yes, I'm talking to you.

You good?

It occurs to me, as such things do, that good people are too quiet.

Too hesitant.

To put their foots forward.

To raise their hands.

To say, "Hey, look at me."

It makes sense.

Humility.

Privacy.

Meek.

As a result of their silence, there's this sea of foot-forwarded, hand-raised, look-at-me jackarses.

Just an ocean of 'em.

An ocean!

An ocean of jackarses.

🌊

So much so that you start believing maybe that's all there is out there.

But I'm here to tell you something.

There's a lot more good out there than not.

I believe that.

Do you?

As a believer, as a father, I must.

The good far outnumber the others.

The good far outnumber the others.

The good far outnumber the others.

It just doesn't seem that way anytime you turn on your technology.

But I hope you see it every time you turn it off.

I went to a store opening the other day.

A fancy little fusion joint.

And it was a soft opening.

A bunch of young folk running around and messing up.

A dress rehearsal in front of a live studio audience.

So eager, optimistic, to get it right.

Nervous, hopeful, and trying.

Giving a darn.

Got an old(ish) man rooting for them.

And for a lot of you.

More than I sometimes think.

To the quiet good.

Get louder.

📣

Thank you for giving a sheet.
